proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib (positive main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab (positive main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have shorter mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel (negative main effect, reflecting selection of docetaxel for higher-risk disease).
Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 3.829 on olaparib (n=5098) vs 3.735 off (n=44902); diff +0.094 months, Welch t-test p=0.0023. Significant positive marginal effect.
Mean pfs_months 3.805 on pembrolizumab (n=2384) vs 3.741 off; diff +0.064 months, p=0.13. Direction positive but not significant marginally.
Mean pfs_months 3.732 on docetaxel vs 3.749 off; diff -0.017 months, p=0.36. No significant marginal effect.
Mean pfs_months 3.728 on enzalutamide vs 3.755 off; diff -0.026 months, p=0.16. Not significant.
Mean pfs_months 3.740 on abiraterone vs 3.746 off; diff -0.006 months, p=0.74. Not significant.
Mean pfs_months 3.741 on lu177_psma vs 3.745 off; diff -0.004 months, p=0.86. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (older patients have worse prognosis).
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant disease) is associated with shorter pfs_months than mcrpc=0.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.856, p<1e-300. Surprising direction: older age is strongly POSITIVELY correlated with PFS in this cohort, opposite to the hypothesized negative direction. Hypothesis refuted in direction.
Pearson r = -0.389, p<1e-300. Strongly negative correlation as predicted. Mean PFS 4.69 (ECOG 0), 3.49 (ECOG 1), 2.38 (ECOG 2).
Pearson r = -0.153, p=3.5e-260. Higher PSA modestly associated with shorter PFS as hypothesized.
Pearson r = +0.123, p=1.5e-166. Higher albumin associated with longer PFS as hypothesized.
Pearson r = -0.143, p=1e-225. Greater weight loss associated with shorter PFS as hypothesized.
Mean PFS 4.031 (mcrpc=0) vs 3.509 (mcrpc=1); diff -0.521 months, p=1.6e-181. mCRPC strongly associated with shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with brca2_mutation=1 (positive treatment-by-biomarker interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in patients with msi_high=1 (positive treatment-by-biomarker interaction).
treatment_lu177_psma improves pfs_months specifically in patients with psma_high=1 (positive treatment-by-biomarker interaction).

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of treatment_olaparib effect within brca2_mutation strata.
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+: mean PFS 5.216 on olaparib (n=529) vs 3.671 off (n=4467); diff +1.545 months, p=4e-27. BRCA2-: 3.668 on (n=4569) vs 3.742 off (n=40435); diff -0.073, p=0.019. Strong positive interaction confirming biomarker-matched benefit.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of treatment_pembrolizumab effect within msi_high strata.
analyses.result_summary: MSI-high: 3.527 on pembrolizumab (n=79) vs 3.792 off (n=1449); diff -0.265, p=0.24 (direction opposite to expected). MSI-low: 3.814 on vs 3.739 off; diff +0.075, p=0.086. Hypothesized positive interaction not supported.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of treatment_lu177_psma effect within psma_high strata.
analyses.result_summary: PSMA-high: 3.743 on lu177_psma vs 3.754 off; diff -0.011, p=0.74. PSMA-low: 3.737 on vs 3.731 off; diff +0.006, p=0.87. No interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for prognostic covariates (age_years, ecog_ps, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, mcrpc), treatment_olaparib has a significant positive interaction with brca2_mutation on pfs_months, while its main effect in brca2_mutation=0 patients is null.

In a multivariable OLS model, the marginal main effects of treatment_enzalutamide, treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, and treatment_lu177_psma on pfs_months are not significantly different from zero (treatments roughly balanced across prognostic risk).
After adjusting for prognostic covariates, brca2_mutation has no main effect on pfs_months independent of olaparib treatment (its prognostic value is conditional on therapy).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ age + ecog + psa + albumin + weight_loss + mcrpc + olaparib + brca2 + olaparib:brca2
analyses.result_summary: olaparib main effect coef = -0.003 (p=0.66); brca2 main effect coef = +0.003 (p=0.63); olaparib:brca2 interaction coef = +1.440 (p<1e-300, t=66.4). The PFS benefit of olaparib is essentially entirely confined to BRCA2-mutated patients.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS with all 6 treatments and prognostic covariates.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients (months PFS): enzalutamide -0.005 (p=0.28), abiraterone +0.0001 (p=0.99), docetaxel -0.006 (p=0.20), lu177_psma -0.007 (p=0.24), pembrolizumab +0.018 (p=0.07). No significant treatment main effects after adjustment.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS with brca2 and olaparib:brca2 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2 main effect coef = +0.003, p=0.63. Adjusted main effect of brca2_mutation on PFS independent of olaparib is null; its impact is essentially via the olaparib interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In BRCA2-mutated patients (brca2_mutation=1), treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months by approximately 1.4 months after adjustment for prognostic covariates.
In BRCA2-wildtype patients (brca2_mutation=0), treatment_olaparib has no significant effect on pfs_months after adjustment (i.e., no off-target benefit, and no off-target harm).
In MSI-high patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does not significantly change pfs_months after adjustment for prognostic covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted olaparib effect in BRCA2+ subgroup = +1.437 months (95% CI 1.36-1.51), p<1e-300. Confirms biomarker-matched benefit.
Adjusted olaparib effect in BRCA2- subgroup = -0.003, p=0.59. The slight negative univariate effect (-0.073) was confounded; no off-target effect after adjustment.
Adjusted pembrolizumab effect in MSI-high = +0.051, p=0.36. No significant benefit in the biomarker-matched subgroup despite expected biological rationale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_enzalutamide has a smaller (less positive or more negative) effect on pfs_months in ar_v7_positive=1 patients than in ar_v7_positive=0 patients (negative interaction).
treatment_abiraterone has a smaller effect on pfs_months in ar_v7_positive=1 patients than in ar_v7_positive=0 patients (negative interaction).
treatment_docetaxel has a more positive effect on pfs_months in patients with visceral_mets=1 than visceral_mets=0 (chemotherapy preferred for high-burden visceral disease).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +0.004, p=0.71. No evidence that AR-V7 status modifies enzalutamide PFS effect; main effect of enzalutamide null in both subgroups.
Interaction coef = +0.014, p=0.21. No significant interaction between abiraterone and AR-V7 status.
Interaction coef = +0.007, p=0.55. No significant interaction; docetaxel does not show enriched benefit in visceral-mets subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).
rural_residence=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than rural_residence=0 patients.
The brca2_mutation x treatment_olaparib PFS benefit is consistent across race_ethnicity strata (no race-by-interaction effect).

analyses.result_summary: Group means: asian 3.797, black 3.741, white 3.741, hispanic 3.737, other 3.749. ANOVA F=0.564, p=0.69. No significant difference by race/ethnicity.
Group means: medicare 3.754, private 3.736, medicaid 3.741, uninsured 3.726. ANOVA F=0.344, p=0.79. No significant difference by insurance.
Rural mean PFS 3.756 vs non-rural 3.741; diff +0.015, p=0.50. No effect of rural residence.

analyses.code: Stratified means of olaparib effect within BRCA2+ patients by race_ethnicity.
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2+ patients: white +1.609, black +1.674, asian +1.544, hispanic +1.024, other +2.958. Magnitude varies but interaction is consistently positive across groups; sample sizes in some strata are small (asian n=30, other n=8). No formal evidence of effect modification by race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 28 SNPs (snp_rs* columns) has a Pearson correlation with pfs_months that survives Bonferroni correction (alpha=0.05/28=0.0018).

Higher comorbidity burden (sum of diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation, prior_malignancy) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment for other prognostic factors.

analyses.code: Pearson r between each snp_rs* column and pfs_months.
analyses.result_summary: Most extreme: snp_rs4986893 r=-0.011, p=0.014. No SNP has p < 0.0018 (Bonferroni threshold). No SNP shows credible signal at multiple-testing corrected level.

analyses.code: Pearson r between comorbidity count (sum of 8 binaries) and pfs_months.
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.005, p=0.25. Comorbidity count is not associated with PFS in this cohort.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient on ldh_u_l in comprehensive model.
analyses.result_summary: ldh_u_l coef = -0.0005 per U/L (p<1e-80). LDH carries an independent negative association with PFS even controlling for ECOG, albumin, weight loss, PSA, mCRPC.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_docetaxel has a less positive effect on pfs_months in patients with ecog_ps=2 (poor PS) than in those with ecog_ps=0 (good PS).
Treatment effects on pfs_months differ by mcrpc status (treatment_olaparib remains effective in both, while AR-targeted treatments have differential effects).
age_years has a different relationship to treatment_abiraterone effectiveness than to treatment_enzalutamide effectiveness (age x abi interaction).

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of docetaxel effect at ecog_ps=0 and ecog_ps=2.
analyses.result_summary: ECOG=0: docetaxel 4.661 vs 4.690, diff -0.029, p=0.36. ECOG=2: docetaxel 2.371 vs 2.378, diff -0.007, p=0.86. No differential effect by ECOG; docetaxel is null in both subgroups.

analyses.code: Stratified univariate t-tests of treatments within mcrpc strata.
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib: mcrpc=0 diff +0.103 (p=0.029), mcrpc=1 diff +0.089 (p=0.040). Enzalutamide: mcrpc=1 diff -0.048 (p=0.049). Docetaxel: mcrpc=0 diff -0.059 (p=0.043). Olaparib remains beneficial in both mCRPC strata; small negative signals for other treatments likely reflect confounding-by-indication.

Abiraterone:age interaction coef = -0.00096 per year, p=0.036. Marginally significant: at older ages the (already small) abiraterone main effect trends slightly more negative. No analogous interaction for enzalutamide (p=0.84).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a comprehensive multivariable OLS model that simultaneously includes all six treatment indicators, four key biomarkers (brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high), three corresponding treatment-by-biomarker interactions (olaparib:brca2, pembrolizumab:msi_high, lu177:psma), and clinical prognostic factors (age, ecog_ps, psa, albumin, weight_loss, mcrpc, visceral_mets, hemoglobin, alkaline_phosphatase, ldh), the only treatment-by-biomarker interaction that is statistically significant is treatment_olaparib x brca2_mutation, with magnitude approximately +1.4 months PFS.
In the comprehensive model, age_years remains the strongest single predictor of pfs_months (positive direction in this synthetic cohort), followed by ecog_ps (negative).

analyses.code: Comprehensive OLS with treatments, biomarkers, three matched interactions, and 10 covariates (R^2=0.952).
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficients: olaparib:brca2 = +1.437 (p<1e-300), pembrolizumab:msi_high = +0.044 (p=0.40), lu177:psma_high = +0.0003 (p=0.98). Only the olaparib:BRCA2 interaction is significant. PSMA-high itself is a weak positive prognostic marker (coef +0.021, p<0.001) independent of treatment. All treatment main effects null (|p|>=0.07). Hypothesis supported.

age_years coef = +0.174 months per year (t=869, p<1e-300). ecog_ps coef = -1.152 months per ECOG point (t=-392). albumin +0.479, weight_loss -0.076, mcrpc -0.376, psa -0.0027 are all also highly significant. Age and ECOG dominate the prognostic signal, with age in an unexpectedly positive direction in this cohort.
